stem: A hiking trail on a mountainside rises 7 meters in elevation over a certain stretch. An engineer models the incline of the trail using the equation sin θ = 7/25, where θ is the angle the trail makes with the horizontal ground. What does the number 25 represent in this equation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The angle of incline of the trail, in degrees

choices.label: B
choices.text: The horizontal distance covered by the trail, in meters

choices.label: C
choices.text: The vertical rise per horizontal meter of the trail

choices.label: D
choices.text: The length of the trail along the slope, in meters

explanation: In a right triangle, sin θ = opposite/hypotenuse. Here the opposite side is the vertical rise (7 meters) and the hypotenuse is the length of the trail along the slope. Since sin θ = 7/25, the denominator 25 represents the hypotenuse, which is the length of the trail measured along the slope, in meters. (For reference, the horizontal distance would be the adjacent side: sqrt(25² − 7²) = sqrt(625 − 49) = sqrt(576) = 24 meters, which is not 25.)

distractorRationale.A: Confuses the ratio value with the angle measurement, interpreting the denominator 25 as the angle in degrees rather than a side length = 25
distractorRationale.B: Identifies 25 as the horizontal ground distance (the adjacent side), confusing the hypotenuse with the adjacent side in the sine ratio; the actual horizontal distance is 24 = 24
distractorRationale.C: Misinterprets the ratio as a rate of change (rise/run) rather than recognizing that sine equals opposite over hypotenuse, treating 25 as a unit rate = 25
distractorRationale.D: Correct: Recognizes that sin θ = opposite/hypotenuse = 7/25, so 25 is the hypotenuse, which represents the sloped length of the trail = 25
